title: rubus subcaudatus
intro: Rubus subcaudatus, also known as the Japanese wineberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in China, where it is valued for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Rubus subcaudatus is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, tinctures, and infusions, and its stems and leaves can be dried or made into a wine to harness its medicinal properties.
